div1.entryFree: Stale, vb. to render stale, to make common and worthless: must not so s. his palm nobly acquired, Troil. II, 3, 201 (O. Edd. staul). to s. it (an old story) a little more, Cor. I, 1, 95 (O. Edd. scale). to s. with ordinary oaths my love to every new protester, Caes. I, 2, 73. out of use and --d by other men, IV, 1, 38. age cannot wither her, nor custom s. her infinite variety, Ant. II, 2, 240.
